text: I’ll inspect the fixture app and use the bomly dependency tools the repo points to, then make the smallest version changes needed and verify with `make test`. After that I’ll document each vulnerable package in `FIXES.md`.

The first scan found Maven subprojects under `fixtures/bigapp` but didn’t choose an execution target from `fixtures/` itself. I’m narrowing to the fixture application root and reading the build entry points before changing versions.

The root build is a Maven reactor under `fixtures/bigapp/grouper-parent`. The scan reports 21 vulnerable packages, with several results truncated, so I’m going to generate the full scan JSON locally and extract only vulnerability findings, ignoring license-policy warnings unless they are tied to a vulnerable package.

I found existing dependency management in the parent POM, including some modern direct pins already present. I’m checking which transitive artifacts still resolve to vulnerable versions and whether dependency management can override them cleanly across the reactor.

Some of the vulnerable versions are hardcoded in module POMs, while others come from transitives that the parent can pin. I’m extracting a package-level list now so the documentation matches the actual scanner output rather than just the first compact summary.

The full JSON scan completed and returned policy failures as expected. I’m parsing the saved JSON for `kind == vulnerability` now; this will give the exact package set and advisory IDs to remediate and document.
`jq` isn’t installed, so I’m using the available runtime tooling to parse the scanner JSON without hand-filtering a huge file. This is just inspection; edits will still go through patches.

tool: /bin/bash -lc "node -e \"const fs=require('fs'); const j=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('/tmp/bomly-grouper-parent.json','utf8')); const rows=[...new Map(j.findings.filter(f=>f.kind==='vulnerability').map(f=>[[f.package.name,f.package.version,f.id,f.severity,f.classification||'',f.fixed_in||'',f.title].join('\\t'),f])).keys()].sort(); console.log(rows.join('\\n')); console.error('count', rows.length, 'packages', new Set(j.findings.filter(f=>f.kind==='vulnerability').map(f=>f.package.name)).size);\""

text: The vulnerable set is broader than the compact output showed: it includes direct pins, managed transitives, and two legacy artifacts where the scanner may not have a patched version in the same coordinate. I’m drilling into those edge cases before deciding whether to pin, exclude/replace, or document no fixed version.

Two packages are confirmed as no fixed release on their current coordinates: `commons-httpclient:commons-httpclient` 3.1 and `commons-lang:commons-lang` 2.6. For the rest, I’m checking the fixed target versions and then I’ll patch the parent/module POMs.

A few advisory records are internally useful but conservative: for example, Jackson has fixes for three advisories at 2.18.8 and one advisory marked no fixed release. I’ll still apply the fixed upgrade where available, and document the remaining no-fix advisory explicitly.

Before editing, I’m checking the exact POM spots that hardcode vulnerable versions. The plan is narrow: update parent properties/dependency management, change the few explicit module versions, and replace the obsolete Bouncy Castle `jdk15on` transitives in the Box module with the already managed `jdk18on` artifacts.

I’m applying the version remediations now. This will touch the parent POM for shared pins, `grouper-ws` for hardcoded Axis2/Jackson artifacts, `grouper-installer` for Commons Compress, and `grouper-box` for jose4j plus Bouncy Castle replacement.
The parent POM now manages the fixed versions. I’m patching the modules that had explicit versions that would otherwise override those pins.
The version edits are in place. I’m running a Maven build validation first so any dependency resolution or compatibility issue shows up before I rescan vulnerabilities.

The reactor is still building. The output includes pre-existing checkstyle parser warnings in the Grouper sources, but Maven continued past them because that check is configured not to fail; I’m waiting for the actual build result.

The reactor build succeeded with tests skipped, matching the root `make test` behavior. I’m rescanning now to see what vulnerabilities remain after the remediations and to catch any pins that didn’t affect Maven resolution.

tool: /bin/bash -lc 'bomly scan --path . --enrich --audit --format json -o json=/tmp/bomly-grouper-parent-after.json >/tmp/bomly-after.out 2>/tmp/bomly-after.err; code=$?; node -e "const fs=require('"'fs'); const j=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('/tmp/bomly-grouper-parent-after.json','utf8')); const vulns=j.findings.filter(f=>f.kind==='vulnerability'); const rows=[...new Map(vulns.map(f=>[[f.package.name,f.package.version,f.id,f.severity,f.title].join('\\t'),f])).keys()].sort(); console.log(rows.join('\\n')); console.error('exit', process.env.CODE, 'vulnerability_findings', rows.length, 'vulnerable_packages', new Set(vulns.map(f=>f.package.name)).size);\" CODE="'$code; exit 0'

text: Most findings cleared, but Netty still has four advisories even at `4.1.133.Final`; the scanner now says 6 vulnerable packages remain. I’m checking Netty’s current fixed versions before adjusting the pin again.
Netty’s remaining advisories are fixed in `4.1.135.Final`; `4.1.137.Final` is not available from the configured repositories. I’m moving the Netty pin to `4.1.135.Final` and rescanning.
Netty is patched. I’m doing another fast build check followed by the scanner diff, then I’ll write `FIXES.md` with the package-by-package record.
The build still succeeds after the Netty adjustment. I’m running the enriched vulnerability scan again to confirm only no-upstream-fix items remain.

tool: /bin/bash -lc 'bomly scan --path . --enrich --audit --format json -o json=/tmp/bomly-grouper-parent-after2.json >/tmp/bomly-after2.out 2>/tmp/bomly-after2.err; code=$?; node -e "const fs=require('"'fs'); const j=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('/tmp/bomly-grouper-parent-after2.json','utf8')); const vulns=j.findings.filter(f=>f.kind==='vulnerability'); const rows=[...new Map(vulns.map(f=>[[f.package.name,f.package.version,f.id,f.severity,f.title].join('\\t'),f])).keys()].sort(); console.log(rows.join('\\n')); console.error('scan_exit', process.argv[1], 'vulnerability_findings', rows.length, 'vulnerable_packages', new Set(vulns.map(f=>f.package.name)).size);\" "'$code; exit 0'

text: Four findings remain and each is currently classified by bomly as `no_fix_upstream`. I’m still doing one sanity check on the suspicious cases where advisory metadata mentions an affected range, so I don’t leave an upgradable package unfixed just because the summary is conservative.

The sanity checks agree with the scanner for the remaining legacy coordinates: I did not find a usable fixed version via the configured Maven repositories. I’m adding `FIXES.md` now with one entry per vulnerable package and explicit no-fix notes for the four remaining findings.
`FIXES.md` is in place with all 21 vulnerable packages from the initial scan. I’m running the required root `make test` now; this rebuilds the fixture reactor with `-DskipTests install`.
`make test` passed. I’m doing a final diff/status check so the closeout can point to exactly what changed and what remains intentionally unresolved.

The final status shows only the expected POM edits plus the new `FIXES.md`; the extra untracked instruction files were already present and left alone. I’m ready to summarize the remediation and validation result.

Implemented the dependency remediation under `fixtures/bigapp` and documented it in [FIXES.md](/tmp/bomly-study-0iza16wj/codex-mcp-bigapp-3-1783755029/repo/FIXES.md:1).

Changed:
- Parent dependency management in [pom.xml](/tmp/bomly-study-0iza16wj/codex-mcp-bigapp-3-1783755029/repo/fixtures/bigapp/grouper-parent/pom.xml:183) for Jackson, Netty, protobuf, Nimbus JOSE JWT, Google OAuth Client, Guava, jose4j, Axis2, Neethi, MIME4J, and Commons Compress.
- Module overrides in `grouper-box`, `grouper-ws`, generated WS client, and `grouper-installer`.
- Replaced vulnerable Bouncy Castle `jdk15on` transitives from Box with managed `jdk18on` artifacts.

Post-remediation bomly scan has 4 remaining vulnerable packages, all explicitly documented as no fixed version / no safe version-only remediation available: `commons-httpclient`, `commons-lang`, `hibernate-core`, and `ion-java`.

Validation passed:
- `make test` at repo root: `BUILD SUCCESS` 
- Enriched bomly rescan confirms all fix-available findings were remediated; only the documented no-fix findings remain.
